hubRow.title: AI Talk Entry Hub
hubRow.description: This page is the public entry for the AI capabilities: establish RAG knowledge chat, resume guidance, and avatar presentation first, then grow into real trial-code access, streaming answers, and admin-side governance.

1.title: RAG knowledge / persona Q&A
1.description: Use the published resume, project history, and future knowledge sources as the basis for recruiter-facing Q&A.

2.title: Resume guidance
2.description: Generate improvement ideas and analysis on top of the current resume structure and future AI workflows.

3.title: Avatar introduction
3.description: Reserve a dedicated media route and contract first, then add real audio/video and avatar interaction later.

profile.nextDescription: The next milestone focuses on trial-code chat, the admin governance loop, and then grows the advisory and avatar capabilities into dedicated workspaces.

front.title: RAG knowledge / persona Q&A
front.description: Turn the published resume and future knowledge sources into a traceable public Q&A entry.

back.title: RAG chat workspace
back.description: Redeem a trial code here, then move into a 10-turn conversation and the streaming workspace.

details.one: Resume data, project history, and future knowledge sources become one retrieval context.
details.two: Answers surface source snippets and remaining quota so the rationale is easy to inspect.

front.title: Resume guidance
front.description: Use the current resume structure and AI analysis to produce more actionable improvement suggestions.

back.title: Resume guidance
back.description: This capability builds on the current resume structure, while the real workflow still lives in the admin workbench.

details.one: Inputs stay grounded in `StandardResume`, published snapshots, and analysis outputs.
details.two: Later it can grow into markdown / PDF output, diff previews, and apply flows.

front.title: Avatar / self-introduction
front.description: A dedicated route for media presentation, self-introduction, and future avatar interaction.

back.title: Avatar introduction
back.description: The avatar grows in its own route. This round keeps the entry, copy, and media boundary in place.

details.one: Later this route can own video, TTS, posters, and media playback state.
details.two: The page will keep expanding around persona data, assets, and control zones.

resumeAdvisorPage.title: Resume guidance
resumeAdvisorPage.description: This is the public explainer route: clarify the capability boundary, input baseline, and advanced direction first, while the real generation workflow stays in admin.

overview.title: What does it do now?
overview.description: It works from the current resume structure, published snapshots, and AI analysis instead of starting over from an unstructured block of text.

workflow.title: How does the first version land?
workflow.description: The base version focuses on suggestion summaries, rewrite directions, and export references. One-click apply and field-level diff flows stay in the advanced phase.

boundary.title: Why is this page only an explainer?
boundary.description: Because the real governance and review loop belongs in admin. The public site currently keeps a lightweight overview so visitors understand the upcoming capability.

entry.title: AI Talk Hub
entry.description: This becomes the public AI entry for the site: establish RAG chat, avatar intro, and session history first, then plug in real trial-code access, streaming Q&A, and knowledge-base governance.

path.title: Establish the entry and sub-route semantics first
path.lineOne: The hub explains what the product can and cannot do before the real conversation backend lands.
path.lineTwo: The real Q&A flow will live in dedicated chat and session workspaces so future interaction complexity does not force a structural rewrite.
path.lineThree: The avatar entry keeps its own page, media contract, and control-zone boundary from day one.

prompts.title: Questions it should answer later
prompts.one: What projects has he focused on in recent years?
prompts.two: Is he more frontend-oriented or full-stack?
prompts.three: What should an interviewer ask about next?

chat.title: RAG Chat
chat.description: Later this is where visitors redeem a trial code, start a 10-turn conversation, and enter the real chat workspace.

avatar.title: Avatar Introduction
avatar.description: A dedicated page for video, TTS, and future media interaction. This round only establishes the page boundary and contract.

sessions.title: Session History
sessions.description: Later every real conversation will get a dedicated session page with remaining turns, source snippets, and history playback.
sessions.helper: This round only keeps the information architecture. The revisit entry arrives once the real chat flow exists.

chat.title: RAG Chat Entry
chat.description: This becomes the true start page for public AI Talk: redeem a trial code first, then begin streaming Q&A and a multi-turn session.

code.title: Trial code redemption
code.description: Later this section accepts a trial code and lets a public visitor start chatting without an admin login.

session.title: Session start
session.description: Once the quota is confirmed, a dedicated session is created and the user moves into the real conversation workspace.

citations.title: Source snippets
citations.description: Answers should expose source snippets, supporting context, and the remaining-turn status as early as possible.

workspacePreview.title: Preview the future session workspace
workspacePreview.description: The real chat pipeline is not wired yet, but the route structure already reserves a dedicated session workspace.

session.title: Session Workspace · {sessionId}
session.description: The real message timeline, cited sources, and remaining-turn status will all live here. For now this page only establishes its responsibility boundary.

messages.title: Message timeline
messages.description: This area later renders user prompts, streaming answers, and feedback actions.

sources.title: Source snippets
sources.description: RAG citations, matched documents, and explanation notes will be grouped here.

quota.title: Quota and state
quota.description: Remaining turns, linked trial-code metadata, and error states will live here for demos and future auditing.

avatar.title: Avatar Introduction
avatar.description: The avatar route will own self-introduction, media playback, and future interaction controls. This round keeps it as a contract-first placeholder.

intro.lineOne: Phase one starts with a short self-introduction and capability framing so visitors know this is not just a static video page.
intro.lineTwo: Later this page can grow into voice, video, or avatar-driven playback without mixing those concerns into the chat workspace.
intro.lineThree: The route is intentionally reserved for persona data, media assets, playback state, and future control panels.

contract.title: Reserve clear boundaries for future media features

role.title: Role metadata
role.description: Reserve structured persona data, intro scripts, and presentation tone for the avatar route.

media.title: Media assets
media.description: Reserve poster, audio, video, and cover slots so real media capabilities can be added later.

controls.title: Control panel
controls.description: Reserve playback, mute, replay, and future action controls instead of pushing them into generic content cards.
